message:
fix(scheduler): use wrapping_add for timer ID counter overflow

Change next_timer_id increment from saturating_add(1) to wrapping_add(1).
With saturating_add, the counter would permanently stick at u64::MAX once
reached, causing all subsequent timers to share the same ID. While u64
overflow is astronomically unlikely in practice, wrapping_add correctly
recycles IDs and is the standard idiom for monotonic counters that don't
need overflow panic semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
